revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Suwa City}} | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai]] | founded = [[:en:1640|1640]] | founder = [[Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of [[Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of [[Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu]], [[Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the [[onsen]] sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in [[Shimosuwa]]. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[Suwa Tadatsune]] to [[Suwa Tadamasa]].
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from [[Takamatsu Castle]] to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by [[:en:Mokujiki|Mokujiki]], stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the [[:en:Battle of Nagashino|Battle of Nagashino]].
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]]'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Suwa City}} | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai]] | founded = [[:en:1640|1640]] | founder = [[Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai]] Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu]], [[Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in [[Shimosuwa]]. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[Suwa Tadatsune]] to [[Suwa Tadamasa]].
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from [[:en:Takamatsu Castle|Takamatsu Castle]] to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by [[:en:Mokujiki|Mokujiki]], stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the [[:en:Battle of Nagashino|Battle of Nagashino]].
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]]'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11562942]] Wikidata name: Onsen-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Suwa City}} | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai]] | founded = [[:en:1640|1640]] | founder = [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai]] Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu]], [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] to [[:en:Suwa Tadamasa|Suwa Tadamasa]].
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from [[:en:Takamatsu Castle|Takamatsu Castle]] to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by [[:en:Mokujiki|Mokujiki]], stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the [[:en:Battle of Nagashino|Battle of Nagashino]].
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]]'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:اونسن جى]][[en:Onsen-ji (Nagano)]][[ja:温泉寺 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562942]] Wikidata name: Onsen-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}} |image = File:Suwa clan Cemetery.jpg | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai]] | founded = [[:en:1640|1640]] | founder = [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station | prefecture= {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates= {{Coord|36.050277777778|N|138.11919444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | part_of= {{ill|Chūbu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|WD=Q11367265}} }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai]] Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu]], [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] to [[:en:Suwa Tadamasa|Suwa Tadamasa]].
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from [[:en:Takamatsu Castle|Takamatsu Castle]] to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by [[:en:Mokujiki|Mokujiki]], stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the [[:en:Battle of Nagashino|Battle of Nagashino]].
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]]'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:اونسن جى]][[en:Onsen-ji (Nagano)]][[ja:温泉寺 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562942]] Wikidata name: Onsen-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}} |image = File:Suwa clan Cemetery.jpg | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai]] | founded = [[:en:1640|1640]] | founder = [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station | prefecture= {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates= {{Coord|36.050277777778|N|138.11919444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | part_of= {{ill|Chūbu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|WD=Q11367265}} }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai]] Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu]], [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] to [[:en:Suwa Tadamasa|Suwa Tadamasa]].
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from [[:en:Takamatsu Castle|Takamatsu Castle]] to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by [[:en:Mokujiki|Mokujiki]], stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the [[:en:Battle of Nagashino|Battle of Nagashino]].
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]]'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:اونسن جى]][[en:Onsen-ji (Nagano)]][[ja:温泉寺 (諏訪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:اونسن جى]][[en:Onsen-ji (Nagano)]][[ja:温泉寺 (諏訪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:اونسن جى]][[en:Onsen-ji (Nagano)]][[ja:温泉寺 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562942]] Wikidata name: Onsen-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}} |image = File:Suwa clan Cemetery.jpg | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai]] | founded = [[:en:1640|1640]] | founder = [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station | prefecture= {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates= {{Coord|36.050277777778|N|138.11919444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | part_of= {{ill|Chūbu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|WD=Q11367265}} }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai]] Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu]], [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] to [[:en:Suwa Tadamasa|Suwa Tadamasa]].
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from [[:en:Takamatsu Castle|Takamatsu Castle]] to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by [[:en:Mokujiki|Mokujiki]], stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the [[:en:Battle of Nagashino|Battle of Nagashino]].
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]]'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from {{ill|Takamatsu Castle|en|Takamatsu Castle|WD=Q1305872|lt=Takamatsu Castle}} to reconstruct the temple grounds. The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} and the surrounding hot spring town.

== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}}'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].

The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in {{ill|1640|en|1640|WD=Q6901|lt=1640}} (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu}}, [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}}. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] to {{ill|Suwa Tadamasa|en|Suwa Tadamasa|WD=Q11631883|lt=Suwa Tadamasa}}.
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from {{ill|Takamatsu Castle|en|Takamatsu Castle|WD=Q1305872|lt=Takamatsu Castle}} to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by {{ill|Mokujiki|en|Mokujiki|WD=Q104876072|lt=Mokujiki}}, stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the {{ill|Battle of Nagashino|en|Battle of Nagashino|WD=Q428369|lt=Battle of Nagashino}}.
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}}'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} |image = File:Suwa clan Cemetery.jpg | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai Sect|1=Rinzai school}}, [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} | founded = {{ill|1640|en|1640|WD=Q6901|lt=1640|1=1640}} | founder = [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station | prefecture= {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338|1=Suwa, Nagano}} | coordinates= {{Coord|36.050277777778|N|138.11919444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | part_of= {{ill|Chūbu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|WD=Q11367265|1=Chūbu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage}} }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai|1=Rinzai school}} Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in {{ill|1640|en|1640|WD=Q6901|lt=1640|1=1640}} (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu|1=Takamatsu Domain}}, [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}}. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] to {{ill|Suwa Tadamasa|en|Suwa Tadamasa|WD=Q11631883|lt=Suwa Tadamasa|1=Suwa Tadamasa}}.
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from {{ill|Takamatsu Castle|en|Takamatsu Castle|WD=Q1305872|lt=Takamatsu Castle|1=Takamatsu Castle}} to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by {{ill|Mokujiki|en|Mokujiki|WD=Q104876072|lt=Mokujiki|1=Mokujiki}}, stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the {{ill|Battle of Nagashino|en|Battle of Nagashino|WD=Q428369|lt=Battle of Nagashino|1=Battle of Nagashino}}.
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = 温泉寺 (Onsen-ji) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}} |image = File:Suwa clan Cemetery.jpg | location = Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | coords = {{Coord|36|3|1|N|138|7|9.1|E|display=inline,title}} | sect = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai Sect|1=Rinzai school}}, [[Myoshin-ji branch]] | deity = {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} | founded = {{ill|1640|en|1640|WD=Q6901|lt=1640|1=1640}} | founder = [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] | address = 1-21-1 Yunowaki, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | cultural_property = [[National Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] (Takamatsu Clan Graveyard) | pilgrimage = [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]], 7th station; [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]], 24th station | prefecture= {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338|1=Suwa, Nagano}} | coordinates= {{Coord|36.050277777778|N|138.11919444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | part_of= {{ill|Chūbu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|WD=Q11367265|1=Chūbu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage}} }} '''Onsen-ji''' (温泉寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai|1=Rinzai school}} Buddhist temple affiliated with the [[Myoshin-ji]] branch, located in the city of {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name is Rinjozan (臨江山), and its principal deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] (Bhaisajyaguru). It serves as the 7th station of the [[Chubu 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage]] and the 24th station of the [[Suwa 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]].
== History == Onsen-ji was established in {{ill|1640|en|1640|WD=Q6901|lt=1640|1=1640}} (Kan'ei 17) by the second lord of {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu|1=Takamatsu Domain}}, [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]], to safeguard the {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} sources of the area. The founding monk, [[Tairei Genmi]], was invited from the [[Jiun-ji Temple|Jiun-ji]] in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}}. The temple became the family temple of the Suwa clan, with the graves of eight generations of clan heads, from [[:en:Suwa Tadatsune|Suwa Tadatsune]] to {{ill|Suwa Tadamasa|en|Suwa Tadamasa|WD=Q11631883|lt=Suwa Tadamasa|1=Suwa Tadamasa}}.
After a fire in 1870 (Meiji 3), which destroyed its main structures, the temple's gates, Noh stage, and Yakui-mon were relocated from {{ill|Takamatsu Castle|en|Takamatsu Castle|WD=Q1305872|lt=Takamatsu Castle|1=Takamatsu Castle}} to reconstruct the temple grounds. The site also features a statue by {{ill|Mokujiki|en|Mokujiki|WD=Q104876072|lt=Mokujiki|1=Mokujiki}}, stone statues by [[Moriya Teiji]], and a bronze bell believed to have been repurposed from the {{ill|Battle of Nagashino|en|Battle of Nagashino|WD=Q428369|lt=Battle of Nagashino|1=Battle of Nagashino}}.
The temple's location on a hill offers panoramic views of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} and the surrounding hot spring town.
== Cultural Assets == * '''National Historic Site''': Takamatsu Clan Graveyard (jointly designated with [[Raigaku-ji]]) * '''Suwa City Cultural Property''': Onsen-ji Iron Pagoda (a multi-storied pagoda used for relics) * '''City Cultural Property''': The pagoda and Noh stage are both historical assets of regional significance.
== Access == Onsen-ji is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}'s [[Kamisuwa Station]].
== External Links == * [https://www.suwakanko.jp/spot/detail/?id=2386 Suwa City Tourism Guide - Onsen-ji] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine-Buddhist Fusion Project]
{{Buddhism-stub}}
